Contracting Authority: Stadt Wien, vertreten durch den Wiener Gesundheitsverbund (WiGeV), Thomas-Klestil-Platz 7, 1030 Wien
Project Description:
- Framework agreement (4 years) for Requirement Engineering services in WiGeV IT projects
- Scope: elicitation, documentation, validation/verification, and management of software requirements; creation of a Lastenheft
- Aim: ensure software optimally meets user needs; develop harmonised Lastenheft usable across WiGeV projects
- Planned partner count: 5 (no exclusivity; no order obligation)
- Known project topics: IS-H Ablöse; KIS NEU; Digitale Kardiologie; Digitale OP-Dokumentation; Nuklearinformationssystem AKH; Laborinformationssystem AKH NEU; Digitale Pathologie; Brückenabteilung; Order Entry AKH; Notfallmedizin; Digitale Fieberkurve; Digitale Onkologie
- Procedure: two-stage negotiated procedure (“kompakt”), above EU thresholds
Important Dates:
- Questions deadline: 6.9.2024, 12:00
- Participation application deadline: 16.9.2024, 12:00 (Einlangen)
- Invitation to submit offers: voraussichtlich KW 42/2024
- Service start: voraussichtlich Jänner 2025

Scope & Requirements.Scope & Requirements:
- Subject: Requirement Engineering (systematic analysis, specification, management of software requirements)
- Deliverables:
  - Requirements elicitation, documentation, review/approval, and change management
  - Creation of a Lastenheft per project; approach for a harmonised Lastenheft across WiGeV
  - Ensuring traceability and stakeholder alignment so software covers user needs
- No lot division (single scope)
- Language for procedure and delivery: German
Scope & Requirements.Contract Volume:
- Duration: 4 years; 5 partners
- Estimated maximum call-offs (ceiling): EUR 20,000,000 (excl. VAT)
- Expected minimum call-offs: EUR 1,700,000 (excl. VAT)
- No order obligation; no exclusivity; direct awards below § 46 BVergG possible
Scope & Requirements.Standards & Certifications:
- CPRE (Certified Professional for Requirements Engineering) or equivalent (scored, not mandatory)
- Compliance: BVergG 2018; Austrian Equal Treatment Act
- Procedure/service language: German
Scope & Requirements.Subcontracting & Consortia:
- Consortia allowed; ARGE with joint liability; name federführer in BEW
- Subcontracting of essential parts allowed; full transfer prohibited
- Stage 1: name only essential subs for reliance; Stage 2: disclose all intended subcontracts (SUL-TNU + SUE; SUS if relying on subs’ finances)
- Reliance on affiliates/third parties allowed (AVU + PAT)
Scope & Requirements.Forms & e-Submission:
- e-Submission only via ANKÖ (www.vergabeportal.at); qualified e-signature (§ 48 Abs 12 BVergG; eIDAS); encryption; no email/fax/post
- ESPD (EEE) allowed (Part IV A–D complete); ANKÖ membership number may evidence eligibility
- File formats: any non-executable; server time applies; German language
- ANKÖ support: +43 1 333 66 66 44; support@ankoe.at (Mon–Thu 7:30–17:00, Fri 7:30–14:30)

Offer Submission Documents: Beilage ./2 Erklärung über den durchschnittlichen jährlichen Umsatzerlös
Formblatt SUS (Solidarhaftungserklärung, falls Reliance auf SU)

Economic & Financial Standing.Minimum Turnover: Avg. annual total turnover (last 3 closed financial years) ≥ EUR 500,000 (excl. VAT); cumulative for consortia

Legal & Registration.Trade/Professional Register Entry:
- Possess all legal authorizations by end of participation deadline (EU/EWR/CH recognized)
- Provide Firmenbuch/GISA or equivalent on request
Legal & Registration.Self-declarations (GWB §§123/124 or equi:
- Absence of exclusion grounds per § 78 BVergG 2018 (checks LSDB/AuslBG)
- Foreign Subsidies Regulation (EU) 2022/2560: ad‑hoc notification of third‑country subsidies on request
Legal & Registration.Subcontractor Identification & Reliance: - Name essential subs (SUL‑TNU) + SUE; reliance allowed with SUS (subs) / PAT (affiliates/third parties); Russian entities excluded throughout supply chain

Technical & Professional Ability.Personnel Profiles:
- Key roles (must be named): Project Manager (./5.1), Deputy (./5.2)
- Minimum 5 years professional experience in the tender scope (each)
- Languages: very good German (spoken/written); good English
- Each key person: ≥1 reference as PM or deputy meeting 4.3.2 minima
- Named personnel must deliver; changes only with client consent; if not employed by bidder: treat as subcontractor (SUL-TNU + SUE)
- Mandatory presence of both key persons at the hearing
Technical & Professional Ability.Headcount / Staffing:
- At least 2 named key persons (PM + Deputy)
- Key persons act as client contacts throughout contract; availability for 15 min presentation + 10 min Q&A hearing
Technical & Professional Ability.Reference Projects: - Key-person references: ≥1 per key role; must cover requirements elicitation/documentation/review/management with Lastenheft; value ≥ EUR 50,000 (excl. VAT); completed within last 3 years; evidence via Beilage ./5.1/./5.2 and Beilage ./6 if applicable

Company Referrals:
- Minimum 3 company references:
  - Subject: requirements elicitation, specification, verification; Lastenheft produced
  - Each contract value ≥ EUR 50,000 (excl. VAT)
  - Completed within last 3 years (completion = final invoice date)
  - If ARGE: bidder’s share ≥ 50% of reference contract value
  - No own-company or affiliated-company references
  - Prove via Beilage ./4; client may contact former clients
Award Criteria:
- Stage 1 selection (max 320 pts):
  - Additional company references (Auswahlreferenzen): up to 5 × 20 = 100 pts (must meet 4.3.2 minima); Beilage ./6
  - Specification of selection refs: healthcare sector: up to 5 × 20 = 100 pts; Beilage ./6
  - Key staff additional project experience: PM up to 5 × 5 + Deputy up to 5 × 5 = 50 pts; Beilage ./6
  - Key staff additional experience (per person): >5–7y: 5 pts; >7–9y: 10 pts; >9y: 15 pts (max 30 pts total); Beilage ./5.1, ./5.2
  - Key staff additional training (per person): Medical informatics degree: 10 pts; CPRE or equivalent: 10 pts (max 40 pts total); Beilage ./5.1, ./5.2
  - Top 7 invited to Stage 2; ex-aequo at rank 7: all invited
- Stage 2 award (max 100 pts):
  - Implementation concept: 50 pts (MUST submit; max 15 A4 pages excl. cover/dividers). Quality judged on completeness, plausibility, practicality, efficiency, traceability, and approach to harmonised Lastenheft; scoring via school-grade mapping (very good=max; good=75%; satisfactory=50%; sufficient=25%; insufficient=0)
  - Online presentation + Q&A (hearing): 10 pts (15 min presentation + 10 min Q&A; only key persons assessed). Presentation quality (max 4 pts); Q&A quality (max 6 pts); grade mapping as above
  - Price: 40 pts; formula: points = (APmin / APAngebot) × 40; rounded to 2 decimals
  - Quality gate: concepts scoring ≤ 25 pts are excluded from price evaluation
